instructions:
Complete the Android Dokka swap. Shorter than the iOS equivalent (0076) because 0078's comparison artifact already triaged the regex-only items into clean categories (vendor code, suppressed internals, legacy ekoapp). The triage concluded all regex-only items are EXTRACTOR_OVERREACH or correctly-suppressed — no real public API is being dropped by Dokka.

## Part A — Final sanity check

Before swapping, do one last verification:

1. Re-read `.docs-ops/evals/android-surface-comparison.md` section 3 (in regex, not in Dokka). Spot-check 5-10 randomly-selected refs. Confirm each is either:
   - Vendor code (third-party lib under amity.* namespace)
   - Suppressed internal package
   - Legacy ekoapp namespace
   - Otherwise clearly not public API
2. If ANY ref looks like real public API the SDK team intends to expose, STOP and report `unexpected_public_api:<ref>` — don't swap; flag for human review.

Expected outcome: triage holds; swap proceeds.

## Part B — Swap android.json source

1. Replace the contents of `.docs-ops/sdk-surface/android.json` with the output of `android-dokka-extractor.py` (schema-compatible output mode — same shape as the regex extractor's output).
2. Mark `android-extractor.py` (the regex one) as DEPRECATED with a header comment — mirror what 0076 did for `ios-extractor.py`. Keep in tree as fallback.

## Part C — Re-validate downstream

1. Re-run the Android accuracy validator: `python3 .docs-ops/validators/android-accuracy-validator.py`. Verify drift is still 0 (or DROPS because false positives from the old surface are gone).
2. Re-run Android doc-as-test: `python3 .docs-ops/integration-tests/android/extract-blocks.py && python3 .docs-ops/integration-tests/android/run-tests.py`. Verify 100% pass+skip still holds.
3. If either gate reports new false positives (mirroring iOS's `String.self` artifact from 0076), apply a small allowlist fix INLINE — same pattern as 0077 did for iOS. Common candidates for Android: Kotlin stdlib (`String`, `Int`, `List`, `Map`, `Result`), Android platform classes (already mostly allowlisted; verify nothing new appears), Java stdlib (`String`, `Optional`, `Throwable`).
4. Run the full local CI gate: `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --base HEAD --skip-fetch`. All 6 checks must remain green.

## Part D — Documentation

Update `.docs-ops/extractors/README.md`:
  - Document the Android migration outcome (mirror the iOS section)
  - Note Android source is now Dokka GFM output (running from `dokkaHtml` task variant; document if a `dokkaJson` task gets added later)
  - Two of four platforms now on Option-2 sources (iOS = ABI, Android = Dokka)
  - TS / Flutter still on regex — sequenced for future migrations

## Out of scope

- TS / Flutter migrations — separate tasks.
- Wiring Dokka task into CI artifact publishing on the SDK side — that's an SDK-team conversation, not docs-ops scope.
- Updating doc-as-test to USE the new signature data — separate future task.
- Schema extension to surface the new richness (parameters, generics) for downstream consumers — separate future task.

## Success criteria

- `android.json` is the Dokka-sourced output.
- Android validator reports `total_issues: 0`.
- Android doc-as-test reports `blocks_failed: 0`.
- Full local CI gate ends in PASS.
- Old `android-extractor.py` is in the tree with a DEPRECATED header comment.

Report in `notes`: the spot-check results from Part A (refs sampled + their classifications); any inline allowlist additions needed in Part C; the before/after Android validator + doc-as-test stats; any cross-platform-portable lessons (e.g., 'expect vendor-code-overreach class to be the dominant Dokka-vs-regex delta — likely same pattern for any platform with regex extractors').

validators.name: android_swap_complete_and_gates_green
validators.command: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/sdk-surface/android.json')); assert d.get('extractor','').startswith('android-dokka'), f'android.json not swapped: extractor={d.get(\"extractor\")}'; print('source ok')" && python3 .docs-ops/extractors/android-dokka-extractor.py > /dev/null 2>&1 && python3 .docs-ops/validators/android-accuracy-validator.py > /dev/null && python3 -c "import json; r=json.load(open('.docs-ops/evals/android-accuracy-drift.json')); assert r['stats']['total_issues']==0, f'android drift now {r[\"stats\"][\"total_issues\"]}; expected 0'; print('OK')"
